title: Business English
description: Essential vocabulary for professional communication, emails, and business settings

cards.meaning: A list of items to discuss in a meeting
cards.example: The meeting agenda was sent out in advance.

cards.meaning: To assign resources to a particular purpose
cards.example: Resources were allocated equally across all departments.

cards.meaning: A formal assessment of performance or value
cards.example: Her annual appraisal highlighted strong leadership skills.

cards.meaning: Profiting from price differences in different markets
cards.example: Currency arbitrage requires fast execution.

cards.meaning: Able to express ideas clearly and effectively
cards.example: A good leader must be articulate and persuasive.

cards.meaning: A standard point of reference for comparison
cards.example: Our performance exceeded the industry benchmark.

cards.meaning: The final net profit or loss of a business
cards.example: Cutting costs directly improves the bottom line.

cards.meaning: The value and perception associated with a brand
cards.example: Apple has enormous brand equity globally.

cards.meaning: The point at which costs equal revenues
cards.example: The project reaches breakeven in 18 months.

cards.meaning: A meeting where information is provided
cards.example: The team received a morning briefing on the new strategy.

cards.meaning: The difference between budgeted and actual spending
cards.example: We reviewed the budget variance in our quarterly report.

cards.meaning: A document justifying a proposed project or investment
cards.example: Please prepare a business case for the new initiative.

cards.meaning: Determining the resources needed to meet demand
cards.example: Capacity planning ensures we can handle peak traffic.

cards.meaning: The hierarchy of authority in an organization
cards.example: Always follow the chain of command for approvals.

cards.meaning: The ability to keep existing customers over time
cards.example: Improving client retention is more cost-effective than acquiring new ones.

cards.meaning: Contacting a potential customer without prior relationship
cards.example: The sales team made 50 cold calls today.

cards.meaning: A backup plan in case something goes wrong
cards.example: Always have a contingency plan for project delays.

cards.meaning: A company's central skill or competitive advantage
cards.example: Innovation is our core competency.

cards.meaning: The values and behaviors that define how a company operates
cards.example: The corporate culture emphasizes transparency and collaboration.

cards.meaning: Achieving good results relative to money spent
cards.example: Digital marketing is more cost-effective than TV advertising.

cards.meaning: The process of gaining new customers
cards.example: Customer acquisition costs have risen 30% this year.

cards.meaning: Making decisions based on data analysis
cards.example: We need to be more data-driven in our marketing strategy.

cards.meaning: Assigning responsibilities to someone else
cards.example: Effective delegation is key to good management.

cards.meaning: Thorough research before a business decision
cards.example: We completed due diligence before the merger.

cards.meaning: Cost advantages gained by increasing production volume
cards.example: Large manufacturers benefit from economies of scale.

cards.meaning: A brief and persuasive speech about a product or idea
cards.example: Practice your elevator pitch before the networking event.

cards.meaning: A brief overview at the start of a business report
cards.example: Always include an executive summary at the top of the proposal.

cards.meaning: Relating to government or company finances
cards.example: The fiscal year ends in December.

cards.meaning: Predicting future business performance
cards.example: Accurate forecasting helps with inventory management.

cards.meaning: The strategy for launching a product to customers
cards.example: Our go-to-market plan targets SMEs first.

cards.meaning: Revenue minus cost of goods sold, as a percentage
cards.example: A higher gross margin means more profit per sale.

cards.meaning: Actively recruiting top talent from other organizations
cards.example: We use a headhunting firm for senior roles.

cards.meaning: Something that encourages a desired behavior
cards.example: Sales bonuses are a common incentive for performance.

cards.meaning: Legally protected creative or inventive works
cards.example: Our software is protected as intellectual property.

cards.meaning: A business partnership between two separate companies
cards.example: The two companies launched a joint venture in Southeast Asia.

cards.meaning: A strategically important customer
cards.example: We assign dedicated managers to each key account.

cards.meaning: Using something to maximum advantage
cards.example: We're leveraging our network to open new markets.

cards.meaning: A product sold below cost to attract customers
cards.example: Supermarkets use bread as a loss leader.

cards.meaning: Increasing a product's share in an existing market
cards.example: Our goal is 30% market penetration in Year 1.

cards.meaning: When a market is fully served with limited new customers
cards.example: The smartphone market is approaching saturation.

cards.meaning: A written message within an organization (memo)
cards.example: The CEO issued a memorandum about the new policy.

cards.meaning: The combining of two companies into one
cards.example: The merger created the world's largest airline.

cards.meaning: A small, specialized segment of the market
cards.example: The company targets a niche market of vegan athletes.

cards.meaning: A legal contract to keep information confidential (NDA)
cards.example: Sign the NDA before reviewing the business plan.

cards.meaning: To integrate a new employee into a company
cards.example: We onboard all new employees with a two-week programme.

cards.meaning: Using fewer resources to achieve the same output
cards.example: Automation improves operational efficiency.

cards.meaning: Hiring external parties to handle tasks
cards.example: They outsource customer support to a third party.

cards.meaning: Ongoing business expenses not directly tied to production
cards.example: Rent and utilities are part of our overhead.

cards.meaning: A specific problem that customers want solved
cards.example: Our software addresses the main pain point of slow reporting.

cards.meaning: A presentation to investors or clients
cards.example: The pitch deck highlighted our product roadmap.

cards.meaning: The process of obtaining goods and services
cards.example: The procurement team negotiates supplier contracts.

cards.meaning: A financial statement showing revenues and expenses (P&L)
cards.example: Review the monthly P&L before the board meeting.

cards.meaning: An evaluation of business performance every three months
cards.example: The quarterly review showed strong growth.

cards.meaning: A document inviting suppliers to submit bids (RFP)
cards.example: We issued an RFP to five software vendors.

cards.meaning: A source of income for a business
cards.example: Subscriptions are a reliable revenue stream.

cards.meaning: Evaluating potential risks and their impacts
cards.example: A risk assessment was completed before the launch.

cards.meaning: Able to grow without increasing costs proportionally
cards.example: The SaaS model is highly scalable.

cards.meaning: The uncontrolled expansion of a project's scope
cards.example: Scope creep delayed the project by three months.

cards.meaning: Any person with an interest in a business
cards.example: All stakeholders were invited to the consultation.

cards.meaning: A cooperative agreement between companies
cards.example: The two airlines formed a strategic alliance.

cards.meaning: The network of organizations in the production process
cards.example: COVID disrupted global supply chains significantly.

cards.meaning: A document detailing a company's environmental and social practices
cards.example: Our annual sustainability report is published in April.

cards.meaning: Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats analysis
cards.example: Conduct a SWOT analysis before entering a new market.

cards.meaning: Cooperation producing a combined effect greater than separate efforts
cards.example: The merger created real synergy between the teams.

cards.meaning: The intended audience for a product or service
cards.example: Our target market is urban millennials aged 25–35.

cards.meaning: A plan to reverse a decline in business performance
cards.example: The new CEO implemented a turnaround strategy.

cards.meaning: What makes your product stand out from competitors (USP)
cards.example: Our USP is 24-hour customer support.

cards.meaning: Encouraging customers to buy a more expensive product
cards.example: Train staff in upselling techniques.

cards.meaning: The reason why a customer should choose your product
cards.example: Our value proposition is simplicity at an affordable price.

cards.meaning: Owning multiple stages of the supply chain
cards.example: Apple's vertical integration controls hardware and software.

cards.meaning: Current assets minus current liabilities for daily operations
cards.example: Healthy working capital ensures smooth daily operations.

cards.meaning: Comparing data from one year to the same period in the previous year
cards.example: Revenue grew 18% year-on-year.

cards.meaning: The act of buying or obtaining a company or asset
cards.example: The acquisition of the startup expanded our product line.

cards.meaning: Return on Investment; the profit relative to the cost
cards.example: The campaign delivered a strong ROI within six months.

cards.meaning: A set of deals or projects in progress
cards.example: We have three major deals in the sales pipeline.

cards.meaning: Following rules, regulations, or standards
cards.example: The company must ensure compliance with data privacy laws.

cards.meaning: A financial debt or legal responsibility
cards.example: The lawsuit created a significant liability for the firm.

cards.meaning: The total income a business generates from sales
cards.example: Annual revenue exceeded twenty million dollars.

cards.meaning: The percentage of customers or employees kept over time
cards.example: Our employee retention rate improved after the pay raise.

cards.meaning: The process of integrating a new employee into a company
cards.example: A structured onboarding process reduces early turnover.

cards.meaning: The gradual loss of employees or customers over time
cards.example: High attrition rates signal poor employee satisfaction.

cards.meaning: A license to operate a business using another company's brand
cards.example: They opened a franchise of a popular fast-food chain.

cards.meaning: The planning and coordination of moving goods and services
cards.example: Logistics is critical in e-commerce fulfillment.

cards.meaning: An important event or achievement in a project
cards.example: Reaching one million users was a major milestone.

cards.meaning: Key Performance Indicator; a metric used to evaluate success
cards.example: Customer satisfaction is one of our main KPIs.

cards.meaning: To expand into new products, markets, or investments
cards.example: The company diversified into renewable energy.

cards.meaning: The rate at which employees leave; or total sales revenue
cards.example: Staff turnover has been unusually high this quarter.

cards.meaning: A document describing a company or investment to potential buyers
cards.example: Investors reviewed the prospectus before buying shares.

cards.meaning: A share of ownership in a company
cards.example: The investor acquired a 20% equity stake in the startup.

cards.meaning: The movement of money into and out of a business
cards.example: Positive cash flow is essential for long-term survival.

cards.meaning: Money owed to a company by its customers
cards.example: We need to chase up our accounts receivable.

cards.meaning: Money a company owes to its suppliers
cards.example: The accounts payable team processes invoices weekly.

cards.meaning: The amount added to cost price to set the selling price
cards.example: The markup on luxury goods can exceed 200%.

cards.meaning: The decrease in value of an asset over time
cards.example: Equipment depreciation is recorded as a business expense.

cards.meaning: To reorganize a company to improve efficiency
cards.example: The firm restructured its sales division last year.

cards.meaning: Reducing the number of employees to cut costs
cards.example: Downsizing affected hundreds of workers at the plant.

cards.meaning: Building professional relationships for business purposes
cards.example: Attending industry events is great for networking.

cards.meaning: The extent to which consumers recognize a brand
cards.example: Social media campaigns boosted brand awareness significantly.

cards.meaning: The portion of a market controlled by a particular company
cards.example: Our market share grew to 25% this year.

cards.meaning: An authoritative report giving information on a business topic
cards.example: We published a white paper on emerging market trends.
